locations.description_short: Young Prince Vorontsov's residence inside the fort where Hadji Murad is received.
locations.dramatic_use: Threshold of surrender; first Russian domestic encounter with the Avar warrior.

locations.description_short: Private chamber in Vorontsov's house where Hadji Murad prays and waits on guard.
locations.dramatic_use: Reveals his hidden fear and distrust beneath diplomatic composure.

locations.description_short: General's house at Vozdvizhensk with study and waiting room where authority is contested.
locations.dramatic_use: Staging ground for the power quarrel between Meller-Zakomelsky and Vorontsov.

locations.description_short: Russian frontier fort on the left flank where Hadji Murad surrenders and Avdeev dies.
locations.dramatic_use: Institutional Russian frontier; collision point of empire, bureaucracy, and mountaineer.

locations.description_short: Seat of General Kozlovsky, commander of the left flank, receiver of Vorontsov's report.
locations.dramatic_use: Off-screen node in Russian command chain; dispatches route through here to Tiflis.

locations.description_short: Caucasian capital and seat of Viceroy Vorontsov; political and social center of the campaign.
locations.dramatic_use: Imperial counterweight to the mountains; polished court masking military failure.

locations.description_short: Small wooden hospital at the fort's entrance where the wounded Avdeev dies.
locations.dramatic_use: Quiet deathbed counterpoint to diplomatic drama; the human cost of empire.

locations.description_short: Forest edge outside Vozdvizhensk where Kurin companies clash with mountaineers.
locations.dramatic_use: Offstage skirmish that claims Avdeev; empire's daily attritional violence.

locations.description_short: Frozen Russian village threshing floor where Avdeev's family flails oats at dawn.
locations.dramatic_use: Rural Russia's rhythm of labor; the home Avdeev is dying for.

locations.description_short: Peasant cottage with stove and frozen windowpanes, the Avdeev family home.
locations.dramatic_use: Domestic Russia in miniature; where the letter is dictated, where grief will land.

locations.description_short: Avdeev's Russian peasant village, implied setting of threshing, hut, church.
locations.dramatic_use: The forgotten home front; prayers and lamentations for the war dead.

locations.description_short: Village church where a requiem is chanted for Peter Avdeev.
locations.dramatic_use: Ritual closure; Orthodox faith folding the soldier's death into routine.

locations.description_short: Provincial town inn where the old man has the letter read aloud.
locations.dramatic_use: Brief waystation showing peasant reliance on literate intermediaries.

locations.description_short: Winter sledge road from the village to town, three sledges of oats.
locations.dramatic_use: Quiet transit of commerce and the doomed letter.

locations.description_short: 1812 battlefield where Vorontsov was credited with conquering Napoleon.
locations.dramatic_use: Backstory prestige; source of the White Cross and imperial legitimacy.

aliases: palace on the south coast of the Crimea
locations.description_short: Vorontsov's lavish Crimean estate built with his wife's wealth.
locations.dramatic_use: Emblem of the Viceroy's opulence and European tastes.

locations.description_short: Russian southern coastal region, site of Vorontsov's palace and garden.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic mark of imperial luxury, far from the Caucasus war.

locations.description_short: Vorontsov's palace in Tiflis: porch, drawing room, cabinet, waiting room, ballroom.
locations.dramatic_use: Theatre of imperial power, flattery, and the first Vorontsov-Hadji Murad interview.

locations.description_short: Palace dining room where thirty guests gossip about Hadji Murad.
locations.dramatic_use: Social stage where the Dargo disaster is re-mythologized as triumph.

locations.description_short: Drawing-room corner where Vorontsov plays ombre after dinner.
locations.dramatic_use: Intimate courtier ritual, snuff-box and Alexander I portrait, arrival of son's letter.

locations.description_short: Mountain village captured by mountaineers in 1843, site of Hadji Murad's past exploit.
locations.dramatic_use: Flashback reference establishing Hadji Murad's battlefield legend.

locations.description_short: Site of the disastrous 1845 Vorontsov campaign disguised as a 'rescue'.
locations.dramatic_use: Hidden shame haunting Vorontsov's court; Hadji Murad's dangerous memory.

aliases: village of Akhmet Khan of Mekhtuli
locations.description_short: Khanate whose widowed khansha Hadji Murad seized in a night raid.
locations.dramatic_use: Anecdote positioning Hadji Murad as audacious avenger.

locations.description_short: Daghestani town Hadji Murad plundered in 1849 in broad daylight.
locations.dramatic_use: Further legend-building episode recounted by Armenian guest.

locations.description_short: Oriental-style Tiflis theater hosting an Italian opera attended by Hadji Murad.
locations.dramatic_use: Exoticized spectacle; Hadji Murad's impassive gaze marks cultural gulf.

locations.description_short: Ballroom with hidden band, champagne buffet, half-dressed women whirling.
locations.dramatic_use: Western decadence confronting Islamic austerity; Hadji Murad's silent indifference.

aliases: the quarters that had been assigned to him
locations.description_short: Lodging assigned to Hadji Murad in Tiflis, reached by phaeton after the ball.
locations.dramatic_use: His gilded cage; escape from the ball into Mohammedan solitude.

locations.description_short: Frontier line in Daghestan from which Hadji Murad wants to raise a revolt.
locations.dramatic_use: Strategic promise; the plan that will never be granted.

locations.description_short: Mountain region Hadji Murad vows to raise against Shamil.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic stake of the war; Hadji Murad's promised battlefield.

locations.description_short: Caucasus mountain homeland holding Hadji Murad's family as Shamil's hostages.
locations.dramatic_use: Absent hinterland exerting moral gravity on every Hadji Murad scene.

locations.description_short: Region Hadji Murad governed in 1839 before being calumniated to Klugenau.
locations.dramatic_use: Backstory origin of Hadji Murad's defection arc.

notes: Grozny and the Lesghian Line appear only as named referents in dispatches/plans (no on-page scene). 'The Mountains' is tracked as an abstract off-screen region because of its persistent dramatic gravity on Hadji Murad's arc; it also appears implicitly in ch.6 via 'come from the mountains'. Kurenkov Village is a composite label for Avdeev's home since Tolstoy names the family (Kurenkovs) but not the village; retained because it anchors ch.8's spatial logic. Dargo, Gergebel, Mekhtuli, Temir Khan Shura, Avaria all surface only as dinner-table/flashback references in chs. 9-10.
